commands.repair_reason: No code repair surfaced; the remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer clearance of the existing human-review pause.; Cleared: The diff removes a narrow private-use citation marker shape before the existing Markdown and DOMPurify sanitizer path and does not change dependencies, auth, routing, secrets, CI, or build execution. (sha=2cf2db4acce853400369d79b9e4734911cddf3e7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Control UI Markdown pre-render sanitizer for OpenAI/Codex citation control markers and a regression test that asserts the marker text is removed from rendered HTML.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: marker-shaped text reaches WebChat `.chat-text` and current main passes it to `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml()` without citation handling. I did not run a live WebChat session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No code repair surfaced; the remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer clearance of the existing human-review pause.

target.title: fix(ui): strip WebChat citation markers
target.body:
## Summary

WebChat rendered unsupported citation control markers such as `citeturn2view0` as raw text inside assistant replies. Those markers can appear when message-tool replies contain citation-aware model markup that WebChat cannot render as links.

This strips only that unsupported citation-token shape before Markdown parsing. Normal Markdown, links, HTML sanitization, transcript storage, routing, gateway auth, and DOMPurify behavior are unchanged.

Fixes #85193.

## Verification

- `ui/src/ui/markdown.ts`
- `ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WebChat chat bubbles displayed raw OpenAI/Codex citation control markers instead of hiding unsupported citation tokens.
Real environment tested: Focused renderer unit coverage in the Control UI markdown sanitizer path.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: The added markdown renderer test covers a citation marker inside assistant text and expects it to be removed before sanitized HTML output.
Observed result after fix: User-visible WebChat output no longer contains the raw `turn...view...` citation marker text.
What was not tested: Browser screenshot verification of a live WebChat conversation with real provider-emitted citation tokens.
target.author: neeravmakwana

commands.author: Takhoffman
